title: Fashion Founders: Sara Blakely of SPANX - Square Magazine
excerpts:
Section Title: Fashion Founders: Sara Blakely of SPANX > ... > **By: Claire Watkiss**
Content:
In 2000, she invested $5,000 of her own personal savings to start her company. She spent zero dollars on advertising, and instead met with boards of various stores to showcase her product. Blakely was unique with her strategies, once even sending a buyer at Neiman Marcus a singular high-heeled shoe in a box with a note that said, “Just trying to get my foot in the door. Can I have a few minutes of your time?”
SPANX took off in late 2000 when Oprah Winfrey featured Blakely’s products on her television show. Blakely continued to model her products on television and in-store appearances to save on costs. Soon thereafter, celebrities across the country were repping the shapewear brand.
After appearing on reality television show “Rebel Billionaire” and winning the grand prize of $750,000, Blakely established The Sara Blakely Foundation, which provides scholarships for ambitious female entrepreneur

title: How Spanx Founder Sara Blakely Went From Selling Fax ...
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > How to Follow Your Intuition with Sara Blakely of SPANX
Content:
In 1998, Sara Blakely took her frustration as a consumer and set out to revolutionize the undergarment industry with SPANX. Using her skills honed in fax machine sales, Blakely acted as the product’s number one salesperson, landing her shaping underwear in Neiman Marcus and Oprah’s Favorite Things. Blakely has been named one of the 100 Most Influential People in the world by TIME, and was featured on the cover of Forbes Magazine as the youngest self-made female billionaire. Twenty-two years after starting SPANX, Blakely sold a majority stake to Blackstone and became Executive Chairwoman. Blakely shares how SPANX has been profitable since day one, why what you don’t know can actually be your greatest asset, and why it’s important to “bucket” your days in order to spend time on what you’re best at.

title: Founder Of Spanx On Courage, Failure And Fashion | Radio Boston
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > Copy the code below to embed the WBUR audio player on your site
Content:
<iframe width="100%" height="124" scrolling="no" frameborder="no" src="https://player.wbur.org/radioboston/2016/12/09/spanx-sara-blakely"></iframe>
Copy embed code
Alison Bruzek
Meghna Chakrabarti
December 09, 2016
Share
Sara Blakely at WBUR. (Robin Lubbock/WBUR)
Sara Blakely was 29 when she founded the shapewear company Spanx. A former door-to-door fax salesperson, she used the $5,000 she'd saved up to create a form of control-top pantyhose that she could wear under her cream-colored slacks.
Now 45, Blakely is the youngest female billionaire in America, according to Forbes. She's also self-made, joining the rarefied air of Oprah Winfrey and Meg Whitman.
Blakely was in Boston to be a keynote speaker at the [Massachusetts Conference For Women](https://www.maconferenceforwomen.org/) , a meeting of 11,000 women in the state. She joined us before her talk to tell us what she's planning next.

title: The Profile Dossier: Sara Blakely, the Self-Made Billionaire
excerpts:
Section Title: ... > “Control your success as much as you can. Do not leave it in the hands of other people.”
Content:
At 27 years old, she found herself at a dead-end job selling fax machines door to door. “I kept feeling like I was in the wrong movie,” she [says](https://www.stitcher.com/podcast/national-public-radio/how-i-built-this/e/46340238) . “I was determined to create a better life for myself.”
One night, she decided to wear her new white pants to a party, but her underwear left unflattering panty lines. So she cut the feet off a pair of pantyhose and turned them into a slimming undergarment. “The moment I saw how good my butt looked, I was like, ‘Thank you, God, *this* is my opportunity,” she says in the book *Getting There.*
